title: Context Retention — member states two intents at once at the delivery step
defect: When the member says two things at once, the VA handles one and silently drops the other.
summary: Faithful reduction of the UAT-007 call log to the exact turn where the defect fires.
Agent asks 'fax or email?'. Member answers the delivery method AND asks to update
their ZIP in the same breath. The single-intent WorkerResult schema can only carry
the delivery_method, so the ZIP-update request is invisible to the agent and is
never acknowledged or actioned. The member then confirms the fax and the provider
list is dispatched against the disputed ZIP (94107) that the member tried to change.

messages.content: Your in-network provider list is ready. Would you like it sent by fax or email?

turns.user: Fax, but I need to update my ZIP code.
turns.note: The ZIP-update request is NOT representable in WorkerResult — only delivery_method is captured.
turns.expected_behavior_target: Acknowledge BOTH: capture delivery_method=fax AND hold/return to the ZIP-update request.
turns.actual_behavior_today: Captures delivery_method=fax, asks to confirm the fax on file, never mentions the ZIP.

turns.expected_behavior_target: Provider list dispatched against the CORRECTED ZIP (or the member is re-asked for it first).
turns.actual_behavior_today: Provider list dispatched against the stale, disputed ZIP 94107; ZIP never updated.

known_failures.description: The ZIP-update request is never acknowledged (silent drop).
known_failures.baseline_assertion: After turn 0 the agent's reply mentions the fax on file but never the ZIP, and no update_zip_code tool call is made for the entire run.

known_failures.description: The provider list is dispatched on the disputed ZIP (94107).
known_failures.baseline_assertion: dispatch_provider_list is called exactly once with zip_code == '94107'; state.zip_code is still '94107' at end.
